oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Healthy “mini” herds and flocks: Pathogen management and antibiotic stewardship for homesteaders and smallholders
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Higgins, Jon E.
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: smallholders
antibiotics
stewardship
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: A segment of bovine and food animal practice is serving the needs of non-commercial, or smaller commercial livestock operations. Their needs are different than the typical dairy, cow-calf, or feedlot operation, but equal opportunities to edu­cate as to livestock management and responsible use of medica­tion that keeps both the livestock productive, and in the public health realm, owners healthy.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; No. 1 (2026): 2026 Recent Graduate Conference Proceedings; 91-92
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
